entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop, Sorghum bicolor, studied for biochemical changes during seed development

entities.name: GRAIN DRY-WEIGHT ACCUMULATION
entities.description: The increase in the dry weight of grains during development

entities.name: SOLUBLE SUGARS
entities.description: The content of soluble sugars in developing sorghum grains

entities.name: STARCH
entities.description: The content and accumulation of starch in developing sorghum grains

entities.name: PROTEIN
entities.description: The content and accumulation of protein in developing sorghum grains

entities.name: FAT
entities.description: The content and accumulation of fat in developing sorghum grains

entities.name: ASH
entities.description: The content of ash in developing sorghum grains

entities.name: HIGH-LYSINE
entities.description: A trait characterized by increased lysine content in the grain

entities.name: P721
entities.description: A high-lysine mutant cultivar of sorghum

entities.name: IS11167
entities.description: A high-lysine Ethiopian line of sorghum

entities.name: IS11758
entities.description: A high-lysine Ethiopian line of sorghum

entities.name: ETHIOPIA
entities.description: The country of origin for sorghum lines IS11167, IS11758, and RY49

entities.name: SEED DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The process of grain growth and biochemical change in sorghum

entities.name: MATURATION
entities.description: The phases of grain development leading to maturity

entities.name: FLOWERING
entities.description: The biological stage after which fat accumulation is measured

relationships.description: Biochemical changes were investigated during seed development in sorghum
P721 is a mutant cultivar of sorghum
IS11167 is a line of sorghum
IS11758 is a line of sorghum
RY49 is a cultivar of sorghum
P721 is a high-lysine mutant
IS11167 is a high-lysine line
IS11758 is a high-lysine line
P721 exhibits relatively low starch content
P721 exhibits relatively high protein content
IS11167 exhibits relatively low starch content
IS11167 exhibits relatively high protein content
IS11758 exhibits relatively low starch content
IS11758 exhibits relatively high protein content
RY49 has a high rate of protein accumulation during maturation
RY49 has a high rate of fat accumulation
IS11167 has a high rate of fat accumulation
RY49 originates from Ethiopia
IS11167 originates from Ethiopia
IS11758 originates from Ethiopia
Protein accumulation may occur at the expense of starch accumulation
Fat content increases for up to 28 days after flowering
